opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've been tired for months, but since this morning I can barely walk to the bathroom without losing my breath and feeling my heart race.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The tiredness has been going on for ages, but it suddenly got much worse today and now any little effort leaves me puffed out with my heart pounding.

symptom_narrative.onset: gradual tiredness over about 6 months, then a sudden sharp worsening since this morning
symptom_narrative.duration: months of tiredness; the severe part has come on over the last 12 hours or so
symptom_narrative.character: worn out and drained all the time, and now getting badly out of breath and feeling my heart racing with the smallest effort
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has ever been — I can hardly get across the room
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the tiredness is my whole body; the breathlessness and pounding are in my chest when I move
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time, much worse with any movement, a bit better when I sit still
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting or lying still and resting
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: walking, standing up, or doing anything at all
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: shortness of breath on minimal effort, heart racing/pounding, feeling weak and a bit unsteady when I stand
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've been tired for about six months; I tried iron tablets for three months but they made no difference, so I stopped them last week
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I'm normally independent and get around with my stick, but today I've been stuck in bed and the chair

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-standing severe rheumatoid arthritis for about 10 years
past_medical_history.how_managed: I take methotrexate and a steroid tablet for it

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives with my spouse who is here helping me; I normally walk with a stick; there are rugs around the house that I keep catching my feet on

objective_data_offered.value: my spouse says I look really pale — my lips and the inside of my lower eyelids look washed out
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: spouse's visual impression over a chat, not a clinician assessment

objective_data_offered.value: my spouse says I get puffed and have to stop after only a few steps to the bathroom
